pattern: Greedily place the largest remaining letter as many times as the limit allows, and whenever that letter still has copies left over, borrow a single copy of the next largest distinct letter to break the run before resuming.
hints: To be lexicographically largest, always prefer placing the biggest available letter first, and only ever fall back to a smaller one when forced.

You are forced to fall back exactly when the biggest letter still has copies left after using up to repeatLimit of them in a row -- at that point insert a single copy of the next-largest distinct letter to break the run, then go back to the biggest letter.
If, after using the letter up to the limit, no smaller letter remains to break the run, stop entirely -- any leftover copies of that letter cannot be placed and are simply left unused.

edge_inputs.description: mixed letters, some characters left unused
repeatLimit equal to the whole string's length, no break needed
no smaller letter exists to break the run, so leftover copies are discarded
limit of one forces strict alternation between the only two letters
three distinct letters each needing a break to continue their run
every letter distinct, limit far larger than any run

stress.description: random lowercase string with a random repeatLimit
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    limit = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [s, limit]
